14454.title: Add codespell support (config, workflow to detect/not fix) and make it fix some typos
14454.author: yarikoptic
14454.last_developer_comment_body:
> > * Sign DCO
> 
> done

Thanks

> > * We'd need codespell action pinning
> 
> do you use any helper for that, e.g. smth like https://www.npmjs.com/package/pin-github-action

No, we just pin manually.

> > * We'd need codegen to pass,
> 
> it seems that test fails are due to interaction with other components of argo [which still carry those typos, like `conditons`](https://github.com/search?q=org%3Aargoproj%20conditons&type=code)... yet to see what the "sequence" of fixes across repos to be, please advise

This would require argo-events to be fixed, then a release made, and then us to import that new release in go.mod. (Technically the release could be skipped and we import as a SHA, but for spelling only that feels like it does more harm than good).

> > which probably means that the Makefile needs to learn to codespell. `make -B codegen` inside a devcontainer is the easiest way to see what this will do. It might be necessary to change which files get checked. Ideally codespell would learn not to check generated files - all ours should be detectable.
> 
> ATM there is no (easy/built-in) way to skip files based on content (e.g. matching "Code generated by"), could only easily skip based on matching filenames (e.g. `*_generated*`)... but also raises the question: why generated files should have typos?

If generated files have typos but they're imported typos (e.g. we're embedding a documented struct from another project as we do) we can't fix them.

The non-generated file typos should be detected and then the generated files regenerated to be fixed also.

> > * Even if you don't need to make the Makefile know how to codespell for codegen, I'd still want a Makefile target like that so I can know my code passes before pushing.
> 
> done, there is `codespell` target now. Should it be added to `lint` or `pre-commit` dependencies?

Yes, this must be in the dependencies for pre-commit.

In order that this is satisfactory it should get installed, and we do this (somewhat badly) via the Makefile, so that you can spin up a devcontainer and run `make pre-commit` and it just works - installing the tools it needs along the way.

181.title: Add codespell support (config, workflow to detect/not fix) and make it fix few typos
181.author: yarikoptic
181.last_developer_comment_body:
@yarikoptic Thanks for your work on this. It was useful to see how a tool such as codespell could be included in CI and obviously, by using it, you have helped us fix a number of typos. When it comes to this PR for adding it to the CI process I'm afraid we have decided not to include that but have moved the additional typos found in this PR to the other one. There is nothing wrong with the general idea but here are the reasons we don't want in include it at this point in time:

1. This repo is not likely to be very active and now that the identified typos have been fixed, there would be little benefit to including codespell in CI.
2. We want to avoid any additional maintenance overhead so if there isn't sufficient benefit we don't want to add new tools.
3. Lastly, as you suggested, it is hard to make this robust. Your regex was a good idea but it didn't catch multi-line text that ends with a language tag so it still made mistakes in non-English text. To maintain this tool in CI we would need to run it locally, identify cases it missed and modify the config. That diminishes the benefit of running it in CI.

Thanks again for your contribution and the improvements you have made to the quality of this repo.

I guess this is the same tool as have been somewhat considered previously in the following links, correct?
- https://github.com/rclone/rclone/pull/6380
- https://github.com/rclone/rclone/issues/4671

We do actually run a spell checker already, `misspell`, which I added myself here:
- https://github.com/rclone/rclone/pull/6412

The advantage of misspell is that it is the only one included with golangci-lint, which we already use for a lot of our linters.  However misspell seem a bit limited in what it catches, maybe there is some configuration that could be done to improve it,  however there also does not to be much development on this tool anymore. Would be nice if codespell could also be integrated in golangci-lint, you don't know if this has been / can be done, do you?

- Some of the files currently changed are generated, e.g. README.*, rclone.1. I see they seem to be ignored in .codespellrc configuration, so maybe its just the order of when tings were done, and will be fixed by itself by a later rerun/squashing/something..
